1: class NotifyCommentJob < ApplicationJob
2:   queue_as :default
3: 
4:   def perform(*comments)
5:     comments.each do |comment|
6:       deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
7:     end
8:   end
9: 
10:   def deliver_comment_notifications(comment)
11:     notified = deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
12:     deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
13:   end
14: 
15:   def deliver_mention_notifications(comment, notified)
16:     mentions = comment.comment.scan(/\B[@~]([\w-]+)/).flatten.uniq
17:     # Remove username of author and anyone already notified about the reply.
18:     # If they have email_replies off, a reply that @mentions them will not generate a
19:     # mention email. email_replies trumps email_mentions to minimize unwanted emails.
20:     to_notify = mentions - [comment.user.username] - notified
21: 
22:     # every user gets a Notification, which may be filtered out from those views so that unhiding a
23:     # story reveals the notifications
24:     to_notify = User.active.where(username: to_notify)
25:     to_notify.find_each do |u|
26:       u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
27:     end
28: 
29:     # but there's no recalling an email or pushover, so sending those has to reflect story hiding
30:     not_hiding_users = to_notify.left_outer_joins(:hidings).where(hidden_stories: {id: nil})
31:     not_hiding_users.find_each do |u|
32:       if u.email_mentions?
33:         begin
34:           EmailReplyMailer.mention(comment, u).deliver_now
35:         rescue => e
36:           # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
37:         end
38:       end
39: 
40:       if u.pushover_mentions?
41:         u.pushover!(
42:           title: "#{Rails.application.name} mention by " \
43:             "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
44:           message: comment.comment,
45:           url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
46:           url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
47:         )
48:       end
49:     end
50:   end
51: 
52:   def users_following_thread(comment)
53:     users_following_thread = Set.new
54:     if comment.user.id != comment.story.user.id && comment.story.user_is_following
55:       users_following_thread << comment.story.user
56:     end
57: 
58:     if comment.parent_comment_id &&
59:         (u = comment.parent_comment.try(:user)) &&
60:         u.id != comment.user.id &&
61:         u.is_active?
62:       users_following_thread << u
63:     end
64: 
65:     users_following_thread
66:   end
67: 
68:   def deliver_reply_notifications(comment)
69:     notified = []
70: 
71:     to_notify = users_following_thread(comment)
72:     to_notify.each do |u|
73:       u.notifications.create(notifiable: comment)
74:       notified << u.username
75:     end
76: 
77:     hiding_users = HiddenStory.where(story: comment.story).pluck(:user_id)
78:     to_notify.each do |u|
79:       next if hiding_users.include? u.id
80: 
81:       if u.email_replies?
82:         begin
83:           EmailReplyMailer.reply(comment, u).deliver_now
84:         rescue => e
85:           # Rails.logger.error "error e-mailing #{u.email}: #{e}"
86:         end
87:       end
88: 
89:       if u.pushover_replies?
90:         u.pushover!(
91:           title: "#{Rails.application.name} reply from " \
92:             "#{comment.user.username} on #{comment.story.title}",
93:           message: comment.comment,
94:           url: Routes.comment_target_url(comment),
95:           url_title: "Reply to #{comment.user.username}"
96:         )
97:       end
98:     end
99: 
100:     notified
101:   end
102: end

3: class InboxMailbox < ApplicationMailbox
4:   before_processing :required_info
5: 
6:   def process
7:     c = Comment.new(user: sending_user, comment: @decoded, is_from_email: true)
8:     if parent.is_a?(Comment)
9:       c.story_id = parent.story_id
10:       c.parent_comment_id = parent.id
11:     else
12:       c.story_id = parent.id
13:     end
14: 
15:     # If we fail to parse the comment, just throw an exception.
16:     c.save!
17:   end
18: 
19:   private
20: 
21:   def forcibly_convert_to_utf8(string)
22:     if string.encoding == Encoding::UTF_8 && string.valid_encoding?
23:       return string
24:     end
25: 
26:     string.b.encode(
27:       Encoding::UTF_8,
28:       invalid: :replace,
29:       undef: :replace,
30:       replace: "?"
31:     )
32:   end
33: 
34:   def parent
35:     return @parent if @parent
36: 
37:     # Emails In-Reply-To formatted like
38:     # story.<short_id>.<timestamp of story creation>@<app domain>
39:     # We throw away the timestamp, since we only care about the short ID.
40:     irt = mail.in_reply_to.to_s.gsub(/[^A-Za-z0-9@.]/, "")
41: 
42:     if (m = irt.match(/^comment\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
43:       @parent = Comment.where(short_id: m[1]).first
44:     elsif (m = irt.match(/^story\.([^.]+)\.\d+@/))
45:       @parent = Story.where(short_id: m[1]).first
46:     end
47: 
48:     @parent
49:   end
50: 
51:   def required_info
52:     body = if mail.content_type.blank? # old, non-multipart message
53:       mail.decoded.to_s
54:     elsif /text\/plain/.match?(mail.content_type.to_s)
55:       mail.body.to_s
56:     elsif mail.multipart?
57:       # parts[0] - multipart/alternative
58:       #  parts[0].parts[0] - text/plain
59:       #  parts[0].parts[1] - text/html
60:       if (found = mail.parts.first.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }
61:          ).any?
62:         found.first.body.to_s
63:       elsif (found = mail.parts.select { |p| p.content_type.match(/text\/plain/i) }).any?
64:         found.first.body.to_s
65:       else
66:         bounced!
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     @decoded = tidy(forcibly_convert_to_utf8(body))
71:     if @decoded == "" || sending_user.nil? || parent.nil?
72:       # We could email the user about the bounce, but that doesn't seem
73:       # to align with current behaviour.
74:       bounced!
75:     end
76:   end
77: 
78:   def user_token
79:     (mail.to.find { |e| e =~ /^#{Rails.application.shortname}-/ } || "")[/-([^@]*)@/, 1]
80:   end
81: 
82:   def sending_user
83:     return @sending_user if @sending_user
84: 
85:     if (user = User.find_by(mailing_list_token: user_token)) &&
86:         user.is_active?
87:       @sending_user = user
88:       user
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   def tidy(body)
93:     body.gsub(/^-- \n.+\z/m, "")                # sig
94:       .gsub(/^(On|on|at) .*\n\n?(>.*\n)+/m, "") # top-posting
95:       .strip
96:   end
97: end

3: class ModerationsController < ApplicationController
4:   ENTRIES_PER_PAGE = 50
5: 
6:   before_action :show_title_h1
7: 
8:   def index
9:     @title = "Moderation Log"
10:     @moderators = ["(All)", "(Moderators)", "(Users)"] + User.moderators.map(&:username)
11: 
12:     @moderator = moderation_params.fetch("moderator", "(All)")
13:     @what = {
14:       stories: moderation_params.dig(:what, :stories),
15:       comments: moderation_params.dig(:what, :comments),
16:       tags: moderation_params.dig(:what, :tags),
17:       users: moderation_params.dig(:what, :users),
18:       domains: moderation_params.dig(:what, :domains),
19:       origins: moderation_params.dig(:what, :origins),
20:       categories: moderation_params.dig(:what, :categories)
21:     }
22:     @what.transform_values! { true } if @what.values.none?
23: 
24:     @moderations = Moderation.all.eager_load(:moderator,
25:       :story,
26:       {comment: [:story, :user]},
27:       :tag,
28:       :user,
29:       :domain,
30:       :origin,
31:       :category)
32: 
33:     # filter based on target
34:     @moderations = case @moderator
35:     when "(All)"
36:       @moderations
37:     when "(Moderators)"
38:       @moderations.where.not(moderator_user_id: nil)
39:     when "(Users)"
40:       @moderations.where(is_from_suggestions: true)
41:     else
42:       @moderations.joins(:moderator).where(users: {username: @moderator})
43:     end
44: 
45:     # filter based on type of thing moderated
46:     @what.each do |type, checked|
47:       next if checked
48:       @moderations = @moderations.where("#{type.to_s.singularize}_id": nil)
49:     end
50: 
51:     # paginate
52:     @pages = helpers.page_count(@moderations.count, ENTRIES_PER_PAGE)
53:     @page = moderation_params.fetch(:page) { 1 }.to_i
54: 
55:     if @page <= 0 || @page > (2**32) || (@pages > 0 && @page > @pages)
56:       raise ActionController::RoutingError.new("page out of bounds")
57:     end
58: 
59:     @moderations = @moderations
60:       .offset((@page - 1) * ENTRIES_PER_PAGE)
61:       .order("moderations.created_at desc")
62:       .limit(ENTRIES_PER_PAGE)
63:   end
64: 
65:   private
66: 
67:   def moderation_params
68:     @moderation_params ||= params.permit(:moderator, :page,
69:       what: %i[stories comments tags users domains origins categories])
70:   end
71: end

create_table "stories", id: { type: :bigint, unsigned: true }, charset: "utf8mb4", collation: "utf8mb4_general_ci", force: :cascade do |t|
    t.datetime "created_at", precision: nil
    t.bigint "user_id", null: false, unsigned: true
    t.string "url", limit: 250, default: ""
    t.string "normalized_url"
    t.string "title", limit: 150, default: "", null: false
    t.text "description"
    t.string "short_id", limit: 6, default: "", null: false
    t.boolean "is_deleted", default: false, null: false
    t.integer "score", default: 1, null: false
    t.integer "flags", default: 0, null: false, unsigned: true
    t.boolean "is_moderated", default: false, null: false
    t.decimal "hotness", precision: 20, scale: 10, default: "0.0", null: false
    t.text "markeddown_description", size: :medium
    t.integer "comments_count", default: 0, null: false
    t.bigint "merged_story_id", unsigned: true
    t.datetime "unavailable_at", precision: nil
    t.string "twitter_id", limit: 20
    t.boolean "user_is_author", default: false, null: false
    t.boolean "user_is_following", default: false, null: false
    t.bigint "domain_id"
    t.string "mastodon_id", limit: 25
    t.bigint "origin_id"
    t.datetime "last_comment_at"
    t.integer "stories_count", default: 0, null: false
    t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
    t.datetime "last_edited_at", null: false
    t.string "token", null: false
    t.index ["created_at"], name: "index_stories_on_created_at"
    t.index ["domain_id"], name: "index_stories_on_domain_id"
    t.index ["hotness"], name: "hotness_idx"
    t.index ["id", "is_deleted"], name: "index_stories_on_id_and_is_deleted"
    t.index ["last_comment_at"], name: "index_stories_on_last_comment_at"
    t.index ["mastodon_id"], name: "index_stories_on_mastodon_id"
    t.index ["merged_story_id"], name: "index_stories_on_merged_story_id"
    t.index ["normalized_url"], name: "index_stories_on_normalized_url"
    t.index ["origin_id"], name: "index_stories_on_origin_id"
    t.index ["score"], name: "index_stories_on_score"
    t.index ["short_id"], name: "unique_short_id", unique: true
    t.index ["token"], name: "index_stories_on_token", unique: true
    t.index ["url"], name: "url", length: 191
    t.index ["user_id"], name: "index_stories_on_user_id"
  end

1: # typed: false

3: require "commonmarker"
4: 
5: class Markdowner
6:   COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS = {
7:     extension: {
8:       tagfilter: true,
9:       autolink: true,
10:       strikethrough: true,
11:       header_ids: nil,
12:       shortcodes: nil
13:     },
14:     render: {
15:       escape: true,
16:       hardbreaks: false,
17:       escaped_char_spans: false
18:     }
19:   }
20:   USERNAME_MENTION = /\B([@~]#{User::VALID_USERNAME})\b/o
21: 
22:   # Rerender all markdown (#1627) cached in db columns, a pattern that predates Rails frament
23:   # caching and our adoption of the feature (6ce15c4f in 2025-10). As we build confidence in
24:   # fragment caching we should drop these columns.
25:   def self.rerender_db_markdown!
26:     Comment.all.find_each { |c| c.update_columns markeddown_comment: Markdowner.to_html(c.comment, as_of: c.created_at) }
27:     ModNote.all.find_each { |n| n.update_columns markeddown_note: Markdowner.to_html(n.note, as_of: n.created_at) }
28:     Story.where.not(description: "")
29:       .find_each { |s|
30:         s.update_columns markeddown_description: Markdowner.to_html(
31:           s.description,
32:           allow_images: s.can_have_images?,
33:           as_of: s.created_at
34:         )
35:       }
36:   end
37: 
38:   # allow_images: transform ![](example.com/img.jpg) to a hotlinked image; default is to print a link to the URL
39:   # as_of: what timestamp to add @mention links as of, because users rename; default is nil which is overridden
40:   #        to Time.current for newly created models passing their nil created_at
41:   def self.to_html(text, allow_images: false, as_of: nil)
42:     if text.blank?
43:       return ""
44:     end
45: 
46:     as_of ||= Time.current
47: 
48:     root = Commonmarker.parse(text.to_s, options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS)
49: 
50:     walk_text_nodes(root) { |n| link_username_mentions(n, as_of:) }
51: 
52:     ng = Nokogiri::HTML(root.to_html(
53:       options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS,
54:       plugins: {syntax_highlighter: nil}
55:     ))
56: 
57:     # change <h1>, <h2>, etc. headings to just bold tags
58:     ng.css("h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6").each do |h|
59:       h.name = "strong"
60:     end
61: 
62:     # This should happen before adding rel=ugc to all links
63:     convert_images_to_links(ng) unless allow_images
64: 
65:     # make links have rel=ugc
66:     ng.css("a").each do |h|
67:       h[:rel] = "ugc"
68:     end
69: 
70:     if ng.at_css("body")
71:       ng.at_css("body").inner_html
72:     else
73:       ""
74:     end
75:   end
76: 
77:   def self.to_raw(text)
78:     if text.blank?
79:       return ""
80:     end
81: 
82:     root = Commonmarker.parse(text.to_s.dup.force_encoding("utf-8"), options: COMMONMARKER_OPTIONS)
83: 
84:     root.walk do |node|
85:       if node.type == :image ||
86:           node.type == :link ||
87:           node.type == :emph ||
88:           node.type == :strong ||
89:           node.type == :strikethrough
90:         node.insert_before(node.first_child)
91:         node.delete
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     root.to_commonmark
96:   end
97: 
98:   def self.walk_text_nodes(node, &block)
99:     return if node.type == :link
100:     return block.call(node) if node.type == :text
101: 
102:     node.each do |child|
103:       walk_text_nodes(child, &block)
104:     end
105:   end
106: 
107:   def self.link_username_mentions(node, as_of:)
108:     # This does 1+n queries in username linkification in comments/bios because this works inorder on
109:     # the parse tree rather than running once over the text. Proper fix: loop to find usernames, do
110:     # one lookup, then loop again to manipulate the nodes for usernames that exist.
111: 
112:     while node
113:       return unless node.string_content =~ USERNAME_MENTION
114:       before, user, after = $`, $1, $'
115: 
116:       node.string_content = before
117: 
118:       if Username.where("created_at < ? and (? < renamed_away_at or renamed_away_at is null)", as_of, as_of).exists?(username: user[1..])
119:         link = Commonmarker::Node.new(:link, url: Rails.application.root_url + "~#{user[1..]}")
120:         node.insert_after(link)
121: 
122:         link_text = Commonmarker::Node.new(:text)
123:         link_text.string_content = user
124:         link.append_child(link_text)
125: 
126:         node = link
127:       else
128:         node.string_content += user
129:       end
130: 
131:       if after.length > 0
132:         remainder = Commonmarker::Node.new(:text)
133:         remainder.string_content = after
134:         node.insert_after(remainder)
135: 
136:         node = remainder
137:       else
138:         node = nil
139:       end
140:     end
141:   end
142: 
143:   def self.convert_images_to_links(node)
144:     node.css("img").each do |img|
145:       link = node.create_element("a")
146: 
147:       link["href"], title, alt = img.attributes
148:         .values_at("src", "title", "alt")
149:         .map(&:to_s)
150: 
151:       link.content = [title, alt, link["href"]].find(&:present?)
152: 
153:       img.replace link
154:     end
155:   end
156: end

3: class Utils
4:   # URI.parse is not very lenient, so we can't use it
5:   # requires a . in domain because we don't want people submitting links to internal hostnames
6:   URL_RE = /\A(?<protocol>https?):\/\/(?<domain>(?:[^.\/]+\.)+[a-z-]+)(?<port>:\d+)?(?:\/(?:[\w.~:\/?#\[\]@!$&'()*+,;=%-]*))?\z/i
7: 
8:   # utility that works on stringlikes (for possibly invalid user input + searches)
9:   # If this function changes, you should run Story.refresh_normalized_urls! and Link.refresh_normalized_urls!
10:   def self.normalize(url)
11:     return url if url.blank?
12:     url = url.to_s.dup # in case passed a Url or frozen string
13: 
14:     url.slice! %r{#.*$} # remove anchor
15:     url.slice! %r{/$} # remove trailing slash
16:     url.slice! %r{\.html?$} # remove .htm, .html
17: 
18:     # remove some common "directory index" pages that are commonly served for dirs
19:     url.slice! %r{/index$} # includes index.html? from previous
20:     url.slice! %r{/index\.php}
21:     url.slice! %r{/Default\.aspx$}
22: 
23:     url.slice! %r{https?://} # consider http and https the same
24:     url.sub!(/\Awww\d*\.(.+?\..+)/, '\1') # remove www\d* from domain if the url is not like www10.org
25: 
26:     url, *args = url.split(/[&?]/) # trivia: ?a=1?c=2 is a valid uri
27:     url ||= "" # if original url was just '#', ''.split made url nil
28:     if args.any?
29:       url += "?"
30:       url += args.map { |arg| arg.split("=") }.sort.map { |arg| arg.join("=") }.join("&")
31:     end
32: 
33:     # unify arxiv page and pdf based on their identifier https://arxiv.org/help/arxiv_identifier
34:     url = url.sub %r{^arxiv\.org/(?:abs|html|pdf)/(?<id>\d{4}\.\d{4,5}(?:v\d)?)(?:\.pdf)?}, 'arxiv.org/abs/\k<id>'
35: 
36:     # unify rfc-editor.org pages based on their URL structures:
37:     # https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9338.html
38:     # https://www.rfc-editor.org/info/rfc9338
39:     url = url.sub %r{rfc-editor\.org/(?:rfc|info)/rfc(\d+)[^/]*$}, 'rfc-editor.org/rfc/\1'
40: 
41:     url = url.sub %r{^m\.youtube\.com/}, "youtube.com/"
42:     url = url.sub %r{^youtu\.be/}, "youtube.com/watch?v="
43:     url = url.sub %r{^youtube\.com/.*v=(?<id>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+).*}, 'youtube.com/watch?v=\k<id>'
44:     url.sub(
45:       %r{^youtube\.com/playlist\?.*list=(?<id>[A-Za-z0-9\-_]+).*},
46:       'youtube.com/playlist?list=\k<id>'
47:     )
48:   end
49: 
50:   def self.random_str(len)
51:     str = ""
52:     while str.length < len
53:       chr = OpenSSL::Random.random_bytes(1)
54:       ord = chr.unpack1("C")
55: 
56:       #          0            9              A            Z              a            z
57:       if ord.between?(48, 57) || ord.between?(65, 90) || ord.between?(97, 122)
58:         str += chr
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     str
63:   end
64: 
65:   def self.silence_stream(*streams)
66:     on_hold = streams.collect(&:dup)
67:     streams.each do |stream|
68:       stream.reopen(File::NULL)
69:       stream.sync = true
70:     end
71:     yield
72:   ensure
73:     streams.each_with_index do |stream, i|
74:       stream.reopen(on_hold[i])
75:     end
76:   end
77: end

1: class StoryImage
2:   CACHE_DIR = Rails.public_path.join("story_image").freeze
3:   VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES = %w[
4:     image/png
5:     image/jpeg
6:     image/jpg
7:     image/webp
8:     image/svg+xml
9:   ].freeze
10: 
11:   def initialize(short_id_or_story)
12:     @short_id = short_id_or_story.is_a?(Story) ? short_id_or_story.short_id : short_id_or_story
13:   end
14: 
15:   def path
16:     CACHE_DIR.join("#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png")
17:   end
18: 
19:   def exists?
20:     path.exist?
21:   end
22: 
23:   def generate(url)
24:     return if url.blank?
25: 
26:     card_image_url = find_card_image_url(url)
27:     return if card_image_url.blank?
28: 
29:     story_card_image = generated_card_image(card_image_url)
30: 
31:     if story_card_image.present?
32:       FileUtils.mkdir_p(CACHE_DIR)
33: 
34:       temp_filename = ".#{File.basename(@short_id)}.png"
35:       temp_path = CACHE_DIR.join(temp_filename)
36: 
37:       File.open(temp_path, "wb+") do |f|
38:         f.write story_card_image
39:       end
40: 
41:       File.rename(temp_path, path)
42:     end
43:   end
44: 
45:   private
46: 
47:   def find_card_image_url(url)
48:     response = fetch_url(url)
49:     return nil unless response
50: 
51:     # If it's too large, skip
52:     return nil if response.body.length >= 5.megabytes
53: 
54:     # Clean the content-type
55:     content_type = response["content-type"].to_s.downcase.split(";").first.strip
56: 
57:     # Check for a direct image url
58:     return url if VALID_IMAGE_CONTENT_TYPES.include?(content_type)
59: 
60:     return nil unless content_type == "text/html"
61: 
62:     converted = response.body.force_encoding("utf-8")
63:     parsed = Nokogiri::HTML(converted.to_s)
64: 
65:     # Try <meta property="og:image">
66:     card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[property='og:image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
67: 
68:     # Then try <meta name="image">
69:     if card_image_url.blank?
70:       card_image_url = parsed.at_css("meta[name='image']")&.attributes&.[]("content")&.text
71:     end
72: 
73:     card_image_url
74:   rescue => e
75:     Rails.logger.error "Error finding image URL for story: #{e.message}"
76:     nil
77:   end
78: 
79:   def generated_card_image(url)
80:     res = fetch_url(url)
81:     return nil if res.blank?
82: 
83:     # Limit the image size according to Meta developer docs
84:     # https://developers.facebook.com/docs/sharing/webmasters/images/
85:     return nil if res.body.length >= 8.megabytes
86: 
87:     add_logo(res.body)
88:   rescue => e
89:     Rails.logger.error "Error generating story image: #{e.message}"
90:     nil
91:   end
92: 
93:   def add_logo(image_data)
94:     lobsters_logo = Vips::Image.new_from_file(Rails.public_path.join("touch-icon-144.png").to_s)
95:     image = Vips::Image.new_from_buffer(image_data, "")
96: 
97:     # since the lobsters icon image is a RGB, we need to ensure the image is RGB as well.
98:     image = image.flatten(background: [255, 255, 255]) if image.bands > 3
99: 
100:     # Resize lobsters_logo if it's too big compared to the image
101:     if lobsters_logo.height > image.height / 5
102:       lobsters_logo = lobsters_logo.resize((image.height / 5.0) / lobsters_logo.height)
103:     end
104: 
105:     # If it looks like the logo is mispositioned, it is likely due to social media
106:     # cropping behaviors on preview cards.
107:     #
108:     # References:
109:     # https://docs.joinmastodon.org/entities/PreviewCard/
110:     # https://docs.joinmastodon.org/entities/MediaAttachment/#image
111:     # https://mastodon.online/@mastodonmigration/109440513681868009
112:     combined_image = image.insert(lobsters_logo, 0, image.height - lobsters_logo.height)
113:     combined_image.write_to_buffer(".png")
114:   rescue Vips::Error => e
115:     Rails.logger.error "Vips error while generating image: #{e.message}"
116:     nil
117:   end
118: 
119:   def fetch_url(url)
120:     s = Sponge.new
121:     s.timeout = 3
122:     headers = {
123:       "Referer" => Routes.story_short_id_url(@short_id)
124:     }
125:     s.fetch(url, :get, nil, nil, headers, 3)
126:   end
127: end

1: class SuggestionsController < ApplicationController
2:   include StoryFinder
3: 
4:   before_action :find_story!, only: [:new, :create]
5:   before_action :require_logged_in_user, only: [:new]

7: 
8:   def create
9:     if !@story.can_have_suggestions_from_user?(@user)
10:       flash[:error] = "You are not allowed to offer suggestions on that story."
11:       return redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
12:     end
13: 
14:     story_user = @story.user
15:     inappropriate_tags = Tag
16:       .where(tag: params[:story][:tags])
17:       .reject { |t| t.can_be_applied_by?(story_user) }
18:     if inappropriate_tags.length > 0
19:       tag_error = ""
20:       inappropriate_tags.each do |t|
21:         tag_error += if t.privileged?
22:           "User #{story_user.username} cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} as they are not a " \
23:             "moderator, so it has been removed from your suggestion.\n"
24:         elsif !t.permit_by_new_users?
25:           "User #{story_user.username} cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} due to being a new " \
26:             "user, so it has been removed from your suggestion.\n"
27:         elsif !t.active?
28:           "Cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} because it's inactive"
29:         else
30:           "User #{story_user.username} cannot suggest tag #{t.tag} " \
31:             "so it has been removed from your suggestion.\n"
32:         end
33:       end
34:       tag_error += ""
35:       flash[:error] = tag_error
36:     end
37: 
38:     ostory = @story.dup
39: 
40:     @story.title = params[:story][:title]
41:     if @story.valid?
42:       dsug = false
43:       if @story.title != ostory.title
44:         @story.save_suggested_title_for_user!(@story.title, @user)
45:         dsug = true
46:       end
47: 
48:       sugtags = Tag
49:         .where(tag: params[:story][:tags])
50:         .reject { |t| !t.can_be_applied_by?(story_user) }
51:         .map { |s| s.tag }
52:       if @story.tags.map(&:tag).sort != sugtags.sort
53:         @story.save_suggested_tags_for_user!(sugtags, @user)
54:         dsug = true
55:       end
56: 
57:       if dsug
58:         ostory = @story.reload
59:         flash[:success] = "Your suggested changes have been noted."
60:       end
61:       redirect_to Routes.title_path ostory
62:     else
63:       render action: "suggest"
64:     end
65:   end
66: 
67:   def new
68:     @title = "Suggest Story Changes"
69:     if !@story.can_have_suggestions_from_user?(@user)
70:       flash[:error] = "You are not allowed to offer suggestions on that story."
71:       return redirect_to Routes.title_path @story
72:     end
73: 
74:     if (suggested_tags = @story.suggested_taggings.where(user_id: @user.id)).any?
75:       @story.tags = suggested_tags.map(&:tag)
76:     end
77:     if (tt = @story.suggested_titles.where(user_id: @user.id).first)

79:     end

3: class SearchController < ApplicationController
4:   before_action :show_title_h1
5:   before_action :ignore_searx
6: 
7:   def index
8:     @title = "Search"
9: 
10:     @search = Search.new(search_params, @user)
11: 
12:     if !@user && params[:q].to_s.starts_with?("https://")
13:       flash[:error] = "Sorry, you have to log in to search for a URL. We're getting hammered by a spambot with many thousands of IPs. More info at https://github.com/lobsters/lobsters/issues/1814"
14:       @results = []
15:     else
16:       @results = @search.results
17:     end
18: 
19:     if @user && @search.results
20:       if params[:what] == "stories"
21:         votes = Vote.story_votes_by_user_for_story_ids_hash(@user.id, @search.results.map(&:id))
22:         @search.results.each do |r|
23:           r.current_vote = votes.try(:[], r.id)
24:         end
25:       end
26:       @results = if params[:what] == "comments"
27:         CommentVoteHydrator.new(@search.results, @user)
28:       else
29:         @search.results
30:       end
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   private
35: 
36:   # searx is a meta-search engine, instances send endless garbage traffic to our most-expensive
37:   # endpoint https://github.com/searx/searx/blob/master/searx/settings.yml#L807
38:   # If you are maintaining a searx fork, please don't 'fix' your targeting of this site.
39:   def ignore_searx
40:     return unless params[:utf8] == "✓"
41:     @search = Search.new({results_count: 0}, nil)
42:     @results = []
43:     render :index
44:   end
45: 
46:   def search_params
47:     params.permit(:q, :what, :order, :page, :authenticity_token)
48:   end
49: end

3: class ModNote < ApplicationRecord
4:   extend TimeAgoInWords
5: 
6:   belongs_to :moderator,
7:     class_name: "User",
8:     foreign_key: "moderator_user_id",
9:     inverse_of: :moderations
10:   belongs_to :user,
11:     inverse_of: :mod_notes
12: 
13:   scope :recent, -> { where("created_at >= ?", 1.week.ago).order(created_at: :desc) }
14:   scope :for, ->(user) { includes(:moderator).where(user_id: user).order(created_at: :desc) }
15: 
16:   include Token
17: 
18:   validates :note, :markeddown_note, presence: true, length: {maximum: 65_535}
19: 
20:   delegate :username, to: :user
21: 
22:   after_create -> { ModActivity.create_for! self }
23: 
24:   def username=(username)
25:     self.user_id = nil
26: 
27:     if (u = User.find_by(username: username))
28:       self.user_id = u.id
29:       @username = u.username
30:     else
31:       errors.add(:username, "is not a valid user")
32:     end
33:   end

36:     self[:note] = n
37:     self.markeddown_note = generated_markeddown

41:     Markdowner.to_html(note, as_of: created_at)

43: 
44:   def self.create_from_message(message, moderator)
45:     user = (moderator.id == message.recipient.id && message.author) ?
46:              message.author : message.recipient
47: 
48:     ModNote.create!(
49:       moderator: moderator,
50:       user: user,
51:       created_at: message.created_at,
52:       note: <<~NOTE
53:         *#{message.author ? message.author.username : "(System)"} #{message.hat&.to_txt}-> #{message.recipient.username}*: #{message.subject}
54: 
55:         #{message.body}
56:       NOTE
57:     )
58:   end
59: 
60:   def self.record_reparent!(reparent_user, mod, reason)
61:     create_without_dupe!(
62:       moderator: mod,
63:       user: reparent_user,
64:       note: "Reparented from [#{reparent_user.invited_by_user.username}](#{Routes.user_url reparent_user.invited_by_user}) to #{mod.username} with reason: #{reason}"
65:     )
66: 
67:     create_without_dupe!(
68:       moderator: mod,
69:       user: reparent_user.invited_by_user,
70:       note: "Admin reparented their invitee [#{reparent_user.username}](#{Routes.user_url reparent_user}) to #{mod.username} with reason: #{reason}"
71:     )
72:   end
73: 
74:   def self.tattle_on_banned_login(user)
75:     # rubocop:disable Rails/SaveBang
76:     create(
77:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
78:       user: user,
79:       note: "Attempted to log in while banned."
80:     )
81:     # rubocop:enable Rails/SaveBang
82:   end
83: 
84:   def self.tattle_on_brigading!(story)
85:     create_without_dupe!(
86:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
87:       user: story.user,
88:       created_at: Time.current,
89:       note: "Attempted to submit a project's bug tracker or discussion:\n" \
90:         "- user joined: #{how_long_ago(story.user.created_at)}\n" \
91:         "- url: #{story.url}\n" \
92:         "- title: #{story.title}\n" \
93:         "- user_is_author: #{story.user_is_author}\n" \
94:         "- tags: #{story.tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")}\n" \
95:         "- description: #{story.description}\n"
96:     )
97:   end
98: 
99:   def self.tattle_on_deleted_login(user)
100:     # rubocop:disable Rails/SaveBang
101:     create(
102:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
103:       user: user,
104:       note: "Attempted to log in after deleting their account."
105:     )
106:     # rubocop:enable Rails/SaveBang
107:   end
108: 
109:   def self.tattle_on_invited(redeemer, invitation_code)
110:     invitation = Invitation.find_by(code: invitation_code)
111:     return unless invitation
112:     invitation.update!(used_at: Time.current, new_user: nil)
113: 
114:     sender = invitation.user
115:     create_without_dupe!(
116:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
117:       user: redeemer,
118:       created_at: Time.current,
119:       note: "Attempted to redeem invitation code #{invitation.code} while logged in:\n" \
120:         "- sent by: [#{sender.username}](#{Routes.user_url sender})\n" \
121:         "- created_at: #{invitation.created_at}\n" \
122:         "- used_at: #{invitation.used_at || "unused"}\n" \
123:         "- email: #{invitation.email}\n" \
124:         "- memo: #{invitation.memo}"
125:     )
126:     create_without_dupe!(
127:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
128:       user: sender,
129:       created_at: Time.current,
130:       note: "Sent invitation #{invitation.code} another user tried to redeem while logged in:\n" \
131:         "- attempted redeemer: [#{redeemer.username}](#{Routes.user_url redeemer})\n" \
132:         "- created_at: #{invitation.created_at}\n" \
133:         "- used_at: #{invitation.used_at || "unused"}\n" \
134:         "- email: #{invitation.email}\n" \
135:         "- memo: #{invitation.memo}"
136:     )
137:   end
138: 
139:   def self.tattle_on_max_depth_limit(user, parent_comment)
140:     create_without_dupe!(
141:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
142:       user: user,
143:       created_at: Time.current,
144:       note: "Hit max comment depth replying to [#{parent_comment.short_id}](#{Routes.comment_target_url parent_comment}) " \
145:         "by [#{parent_comment.user.username}](#{Routes.user_url parent_comment.user})"
146:     )
147:   end
148: 
149:   def self.tattle_on_story_domain!(story, reason)
150:     create_without_dupe!(
151:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
152:       user: story.user,
153:       created_at: Time.current,
154:       note: "Attempted to post a story from a #{reason} domain:\n" \
155:         "- user joined: #{how_long_ago(story.user.created_at)}\n" \
156:         "- url: #{story.url}\n" \
157:         "- title: #{story.title}\n" \
158:         "- user_is_author: #{story.user_is_author}\n" \
159:         "- tags: #{story.tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")}\n" \
160:         "- description: #{story.description}\n"
161:     )
162:   end
163: 
164:   def self.tattle_on_traffic_attribution!(story)
165:     create_without_dupe!(
166:       moderator: InactiveUser.inactive_user,
167:       user: story.user,
168:       created_at: Time.current,
169:       note: "Attempted to submit a URL attributing traffic:\n" \
170:         "- user joined: #{how_long_ago(story.user.created_at)}\n" \
171:         "- url: #{story.url}\n" \
172:         "- title: #{story.title}\n" \
173:         "- user_is_author: #{story.user_is_author}\n" \
174:         "- tags: #{story.tags.map(&:tag).join(" ")}\n" \
175:         "- description: #{story.description}\n"
176:     )
177:   end
178: 
179:   # story validations run on preview, check_url_dupe, title fetching
180:   # and save attempts, leading to duplicate notes
181:   def self.create_without_dupe!(attrs)
182:     latest = attrs[:user].mod_notes.last
183:     return latest if latest && attrs[:moderator] == latest.moderator && attrs[:note] == latest.note
184:     ModNote.create!(attrs)
185:   end
186: end

22:         (

1: class Notification < ApplicationRecord
2:   belongs_to :user
3:   belongs_to :notifiable, polymorphic: true
4: 
5:   validates :user_id, uniqueness: {scope: [:notifiable_type, :notifiable_id]}
6:   validates :notifiable_type, presence: true, length: {maximum: 255}
7: 
8:   scope :of_comments, -> { where(notifiable_type: "Comment") }
9:   scope :of_messages, -> { where(notifiable_type: "Message") }
10:   scope :of_mod_mail_messages, -> { where(notifiable_type: "ModMailMessage") }
11:   scope :read, -> { where.not(read_at: nil) }
12:   scope :unread, -> { where(read_at: nil) }
13: 
14:   before_validation on: :create do
15:     self.read_at = Time.current if !should_display?
16:   end
17: 
18:   include Token
19: 
20:   def should_display?
21:     case notifiable
22:     when Message
23:       should_display_message?
24:     when ModMailMessage
25:       true
26:     when Comment
27:       should_display_comment?
28:     end
29:   end
30: 
31:   def should_display_message?
32:     true
33:   end
34: 
35:   def should_display_comment?
36:     return false unless user_wants_notification?
37:     return false unless is_high_quality?
38: 
39:     true
40:   end
41: 
42:   private
43: 
44:   def user_wants_notification?
45:     comment = notifiable
46: 
47:     # Check if this is a mention notification
48:     if comment.comment.match?(Markdowner::USERNAME_MENTION)
49:       return user.inbox_mentions?
50:     end
51: 
52:     # For reply notifications, always show (user settings handled elsewhere)
53:     true
54:   end
55: 
56:   def is_high_quality?
57:     comment = notifiable
58:     story = comment.story
59:     parent_comment = comment.parent_comment
60:     replier_comment_ids = comment.user.comments.filter_map { |c| c.id if c.story_id == story.id }
61: 
62:     bad_properties = {
63:       bad_story: story.score <= story.flags,
64:       is_gone: comment.is_gone?,
65:       bad_comment: comment.score <= comment.flags,
66:       bad_parent_comment: parent_comment.nil? ? false : parent_comment.score <= parent_comment.flags || parent_comment.is_gone?,
67:       user_has_flagged_replier: !user.votes.filter { |v| v.story_id == story.id && v.vote == -1 && replier_comment_ids.include?(v.comment_id) }.empty?,
68:       user_has_hidden_story: !user.hidings.filter { |h| h.story_id == story.id }.empty?,
69:       user_has_filtered_tags_on_story: !(story.tags & user.tag_filter_tags).empty?
70:     }.compact_blank
71: 
72:     bad_properties.empty?
73:   end
74: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: class ApplicationController < ActionController::Base
4:   include IntervalHelper
5:   include Authenticatable
6:   include ApplicationHelper
7: 
8:   protect_from_forgery
9:   prepend_before_action :prepare_exception_notifier # first in case others raise
10:   before_action :heinous_inline_partials, if: -> { Rails.env.development? }
11:   before_action :mini_profiler
12:   before_action :set_traffic_style
13:   before_action :remove_unknown_cookies
14:   after_action :clear_session_cookie
15: 
16:   SESSION_DEFAULT_KEYS = %w[session_id _csrf_token]
17:   # match this in caddy config for bypassing the file cache
18:   TAG_FILTER_COOKIE = :tag_filters
19:   CACHE_PAGE = proc { @user.blank? && cookies[TAG_FILTER_COOKIE].blank? }
20: 
21:   # copied from https://github.com/rack/rack/blob/main/lib/rack/utils.rb
22:   VALID_COOKIE_KEY = /\A[!#$%&'*+\-.\^_`|~0-9a-zA-Z]+\z/
23: 
24:   # Rails misdesign: if the /recent route doesn't support .rss, Rails calls it anyways and then
25:   # raises MissingTemplate when it's not handled, as if the app did something wrong (a prod 500!).
26:   unless Rails.env.development?
27:     rescue_from ActionController::UnknownFormat, ActionView::MissingTemplate do
28:       request.format = :html # required, despite format.any
29:       respond_to do |format|
30:         format.any { render "about/404", status: :not_found, content_type: "text/html" }
31:       end
32:     end
33:   end
34:   rescue_from ActionController::UnpermittedParameters do
35:     respond_to do |format|
36:       format.html { render plain: "400 Unpermitted query or form parameter", status: :bad_request }
37:       format.json { render json: {error: "400 Unpermitted query or form parameter"}, status: :bad_request }
38:     end
39:   end
40:   rescue_from ActionController::ParameterMissing do |exception|
41:     respond_to do |format|
42:       format.html { render plain: "400 #{exception.message}", status: :bad_request }
43:       format.json { render json: {error: exception.message.to_s}, status: :bad_request }
44:     end
45:   end
46:   rescue_from ActionDispatch::Http::MimeNegotiation::InvalidType do
47:     render plain: "fix the mime type in your HTTP_ACCEPT header",
48:       status: :bad_request, content_type: "text/plain"
49:   end
50:   rescue_from ActionDispatch::RemoteIp::IpSpoofAttackError do
51:     render plain: "You have some kind of weird, implausible VPN setup. If you are not doing something naughty, please contact the admin to start debugging.",
52:       status: :bad_request, content_type: "text/plain"
53:   end
54:   rescue_from ActiveRecord::ConnectionNotEstablished, Trilogy::SyscallError::ECONNREFUSED do
55:     render plain: "500 The database is not taking our calls.", status: 500, content_type: "text/plain"
56:   end
57: 
58:   def agent_is_spider?
59:     ua = request.env["HTTP_USER_AGENT"].to_s
60:     ua == "" || ua.match(/(Google|bing|Slack|Twitter)bot|Slurp|crawler|Feedly|FeedParser|RSS/)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def check_for_read_only_mode
64:     if Rails.application.read_only?
65:       flash.now[:error] = "Site is currently in read-only mode."
66:       return redirect_to "/"
67:     end
68: 
69:     true
70:   end
71: 
72:   def remove_unknown_cookies
73:     cookies.each do |key, _value|
74:       next if key == TAG_FILTER_COOKIE.to_s # don't clear tag filters cookie
75:       next if key == Rails.application.config.session_options[:key] # don't clear session cookie
76:       next if key == "__profilin" && (Rails.env.development? || @user&.is_moderator?) # don't clear Rack::MiniProfiler cookie
77:       next unless VALID_COOKIE_KEY.match?(key)
78:       cookies.delete(key)
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   # clear Rails session cookie if not logged in so caddy uses the page cache
83:   # https://ryanfb.xyz/etc/2021/08/29/going_cookie-free_with_rails.html
84:   def clear_session_cookie
85:     if clear_session_cookie?
86:       key = Rails.application.config.session_options[:key] # "lobster_trap"
87:       cookies.delete(key)
88:       request.session_options[:skip] = true
89:     end
90:   end
91: 
92:   def clear_session_cookie?
93:     # If the session has been loaded, it will contain some default keys and not
94:     # be `empty?`
95:     !@user && session.keys.all? { |k| SESSION_DEFAULT_KEYS.include?(k.to_s) }
96:   end
97: 
98:   def find_user_from_rss_token
99:     if !@user && params[:format] == "rss" && params[:token].to_s.present?
100:       @user = User.where(rss_token: params[:token].to_s).first
101:       Telebugs.user id: @user&.token, username: @user&.username, email: @user&.email, ip_address: request.remote_ip
102:     end
103:   end
104: 
105:   def heinous_inline_partials
106:     do_heinous_inline_partial_replacement
107:   end
108: 
109:   def mini_profiler
110:     if @user&.is_moderator?
111:       Rack::MiniProfiler.authorize_request
112:     end
113:   end
114: 
115:   def prepare_exception_notifier
116:     if Rails.application.config.exception_notifier
117:       exception_data = {}
118:       exception_data[:username] = @user.username unless @user.nil?
119:       request.env["exception_notifier.exception_data"] = exception_data
120:     end
121: 
122:     if Rails.application.config.telebugs
123:       Telebugs.context "request", {
124:         requested_path: @requested_path,
125:         original_fullpath: request.original_fullpath,
126:         query_parameters: request.query_parameters, # protected by filter_parameters
127:         request_parameters: request.request_parameters, # protected by filter_parameters
128:         git_head: LOBSTERS_GIT_HEAD
129:       }
130:       Telebugs.user id: nil, username: nil, email: nil, ip_address: request.remote_ip # authenticate_user overwrites
131:     end
132:   end
133: 
134:   # https://web.archive.org/web/20180108083712/http://umaine.edu/lobsterinstitute/files/2011/12/LobsterColorsWeb.pdf
135:   def set_traffic_style
136:     @traffic_intensity = "?"
137:     @traffic_style = "background-color: #ac130d;"
138:     return true if Rails.application.read_only? ||
139:       agent_is_spider? ||
140:       %w[json rss].include?(params[:format])
141:     return if (@traffic_novelty = TrafficHelper.novelty_logo)
142: 
143:     @traffic_intensity = TrafficHelper.cached_current_intensity
144:     # map intensity to 80-255 so there's always a little red
145:     hex = sprintf("%02x", (@traffic_intensity * 1.75 + 80).round)
146:     @traffic_style = "background-color: ##{hex}0000;"
147:     return true unless @user
148: 
149:     color = :red
150:     [
151:       [2_000_000, :blue, "background-color: #0000#{hex};"],
152:       [6, :yellow, "background-color: ##{hex}#{hex}00;"],
153:       [3, :calico, "background: url(data:image/png;base64,) no-repeat center"],
154:       [2, :split, "background: linear-gradient(90deg, ##{hex}0000 50%, #0000#{hex} 50%)"],
155:       [2, :albino, "filter: invert(100%);"]
156:     ].each do |cumulative_odds, name, style|
157:       break unless rand(cumulative_odds) == 0
158:       color = name
159:       @traffic_style = style
160:     end
161:     if color != :red
162:       Rails.logger.info "  Lucky user #{@user.username} saw #{color} logo"
163:     end
164:   end
165: 
166:   def require_no_user_or_redirect
167:     redirect_to "/" if @user
168:   end

171:     @title_h1 = true
172:   end
173: end

3: class Tag < ApplicationRecord
4:   belongs_to :category
5:   has_many :taggings, dependent: :delete_all
6:   has_many :stories, through: :taggings
7:   has_many :tag_filters, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
8:   has_many :filtering_users,
9:     class_name: "User",
10:     through: :tag_filters,
11:     source: :user,
12:     dependent: :delete_all
13:   has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
14:   has_many :suggested_taggings, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
15: 
16:   after_save :log_modifications
17: 
18:   include Token
19: 
20:   attr_accessor :edit_user_id, :stories_count
21:   attr_writer :filtered_count
22: 
23:   validates :tag, length: {maximum: 25}, presence: true,
24:     uniqueness: {case_sensitive: true},
25:     format: {with: /\A[A-Za-z0-9_\-+]+\z/}
26:   validates :description, length: {maximum: 100}
27:   validates :hotness_mod, inclusion: {in: -10..10}
28:   validates :permit_by_new_users, :privileged, :active, :is_media,
29:     inclusion: {in: [true, false]}
30: 
31:   scope :active, -> { where(active: true) }
32:   scope :not_permitted_for_new_users, -> { where(permit_by_new_users: false) }
33:   scope :related, ->(tag) {
34:     active
35:       .joins(:taggings)
36:       .where(taggings: {story_id: Tagging.where(tag: tag).select(:story_id)})
37:       .where.not(id: [tag, 67]) # 67 = programming, the catch-all
38:       .where.not(is_media: true)
39:       .group(:id)
40:       .order(Arel.sql("COUNT(*) desc"))
41:       .limit(8)
42:   }
43: 
44:   def to_param
45:     tag
46:   end
47: 
48:   def self.all_with_filtered_counts_for(user)
49:     counts = TagFilter.group(:tag_id).count
50: 
51:     Tag.active.order(:tag).select { |t| t.can_be_applied_by?(user) }.map { |t|
52:       t.filtered_count = counts[t.id].to_i
53:       t
54:     }
55:   end
56: 
57:   def category_name
58:     category&.category
59:   end
60: 
61:   def category_name=(category)
62:     self.category = Category.find_by category: category
63:   end
64: 
65:   def css_class
66:     "tag tag_#{tag}" << (is_media? ? " tag_is_media" : "")
67:   end
68: 
69:   def user_can_filter?(user)
70:     active? && (!privileged? || user.try(:is_moderator?))
71:   end
72: 
73:   def can_be_applied_by?(user)
74:     if privileged?
75:       !!user.try(:is_moderator?)
76:     # do include tags they can't use so they submit and get error
77:     else
78:       true
79:     end
80:   end
81: 
82:   def filtered_count
83:     @filtered_count ||= TagFilter.where(tag_id: id).count
84:   end
85: 
86:   def log_modifications
87:     Moderation.create do |m|
88:       m.action = if id_previously_changed?
89:         "Created new tag " + attributes.map { |f, c| "with #{f} '#{c}'" }.join(", ")
90:       else
91:         "Updating tag #{tag}, " + saved_changes
92:           .map { |f, c| "changed #{f} from '#{c[0]}' to '#{c[1]}'" }.join(", ")
93:       end
94:       m.moderator_user_id = @edit_user_id
95:       m.tag_id = id
96:     end
97:   end
98:

101:       only: [
102:         :tag,
103:         :description,
104:         :privileged,
105:         :is_media,
106:         :active,
107:         :hotness_mod,
108:         :permit_by_new_user
109:       ]
110:     )
111:     h[:category] = category.category
112:     h
113:   end

1: #!/usr/bin/env ruby
2: 
3: ENV["RAILS_ENV"] ||= "production"
4: 
5: require File.expand_path("../../config/environment", __FILE__)
6: 
7: exit unless Mastodon.enabled? && Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.bot_name
8: 
9: Story.to_mastodon.each_with_index do |s, i|
10:   if i > 0
11:     sleep 2
12:   end
13: 
14:   tags = s.tags.pluck(:tag).map { |t| " #" + t }.join("")
15: 
16:   via = if s.user.mastodon_username.present?
17:     (s.user_is_author? ? " by" : " via") + " @#{s.user.mastodon_username}@#{s.user.mastodon_instance}"
18:   else
19:     " "
20:   end
21: 
22:   link_status = via + " " +
23:     ("X" * Mastodon::LINK_LENGTH) + tags +
24:     (s.url.present? ? "\n" + ("X" * Mastodon::LINK_LENGTH) : "")
25: 
26:   status = via + " " + Routes.story_short_id_url(s) + tags +
27:     (s.url.present? ? "\n" + s.url : "")
28: 
29:   left_len = Mastodon::MAX_STATUS_LENGTH - link_status.length
30: 
31:   title = s.title
32: 
33:   # if there are any urls in the title, they should be sized (at least) to a url
34:   left_len -= title.scan(/[^ ]\.[a-z]{2,10}/i)
35:     .map { |_u| [0, Mastodon::LINK_LENGTH].max }
36:     .inject(:+)
37:     .to_i
38: 
39:   if left_len < -3
40:     left_len = -3
41:   end
42: 
43:   status = if title.bytesize > left_len
44:     title[0, left_len - 3] + "..." + status
45:   else
46:     title + status
47:   end
48: 
49:   begin
50:     res = Mastodon.post(status)
51:     post = JSON.parse(res.body)
52:     s.update_column(:mastodon_id, post["id"])
53:   rescue => e
54:     s.update_column(:mastodon_id, 0)
55:     puts "failed posting story #{s.id} (#{status.inspect}): #{e.inspect}\n#{res.inspect}"
56:     exit
57:   end
58: end

(End of file - total 58 lines)

1: #!/usr/bin/env ruby
2: 
3: require File.expand_path("../../config/environment", __FILE__)
4: 
5: class String
6:   def quoted_printable(encoded_word = false)
7:     string = [self].pack("M")
8: 
9:     return string unless encoded_word
10: 
11:     q_encode_word = ->(w) { "=?UTF-8?Q?#{w}?=" }
12: 
13:     string
14:       # Undo linebreaks from #pack("M") because we'll be adding characters
15:       .gsub("=\n", "")
16:       # Question marks are delimiters in q-encoding so must be escaped
17:       .gsub("?", "=3F")
18:       # Spaces are insignificant in q-encoding so must be escaped
19:       .gsub(/\s+/, " _")
20:       # Take each space-separated word, then q-encode
21:       .split(" ").map(&q_encode_word)
22:       # Recombine words then word wrap at 75 characters
23:       .join(" ").word_wrap(75)
24:       # Compose final string, folding headers per rfc 2822 section 2.2.3
25:       .lines.join("\t")
26:   end
27: 
28:   # like ActionView::Helpers::TextHelper but preserve > and indentation when
29:   # wrapping lines
30:   def word_wrap(len)
31:     split("\n").collect do |line|
32:       if line.length <= len
33:         line
34:       elsif (m = line.match(/^(> ?|   +)(.*)/))
35:         ind = m[1]
36:         if len - ind.length <= 0
37:           ind = "    "
38:         end
39:         m[2].gsub(/(.{1,#{len - ind.length}})(\s+|$)/, "#{ind}\\1\n").strip
40:       else
41:         line.gsub(/(.{1,#{len}})(\s+|$)/, "\\1\n").strip
42:       end
43:     end * "\n"
44:   end
45: end
46: 
47: def story_subject(story, prefix = "")
48:   ss = "#{prefix}#{story.title}"
49: 
50:   story.tags.sort_by(&:tag).each do |t|
51:     ss << " [#{t.tag}]"
52:   end
53: 
54:   ss.quoted_printable(true)
55: end
56: 
57: if __FILE__ == $PROGRAM_NAME
58:   EMAIL_WIDTH = 72
59:   LAST_STORY_KEY = "mailing:last_story_id".freeze
60:   LAST_COMMENT_KEY = "mailing:last_comment_id".freeze
61: 
62:   mailing_list_users = User.where("mailing_list_mode > 0").select(&:is_active?)
63: 
64:   last_story_id = (Keystore.value_for(LAST_STORY_KEY) || Story.last && Story.last.id).to_i
65: 
66:   # Paranoia: only search back three days so that if last_story_id is oddly low we don't start
67:   # sending every story from the beginning of time, or if mailing list mode breaks for more than a
68:   # few days we won't bury them in email.
69:   Story
70:     .where("id > ? AND is_deleted = ? AND created_at >= ?", last_story_id, false, 3.days.ago)
71:     .order(:id)
72:     .each do |s|
73:     StoryText.fill_cache!(s)
74: 
75:     mailing_list_users.each do |u|
76:       if (s.tags.map(&:id) & u.tag_filters.map(&:tag_id)).any?
77:         # story has tags this user has filtered out
78:         next
79:       end
80: 
81:       if s.is_hidden_by_user?(u)
82:         # user has hidden this story
83:         next
84:       end
85: 
86:       list = "#{Rails.application.shortname}-#{u.mailing_list_token}@#{Rails.application.domain}"
87: 
88:       IO.popen(
89:         [{}, "/usr/sbin/sendmail", "-i", "-f", "nobody@#{Rails.application.domain}", u.email],
90:         "w"
91:       ) do |mail|
92:         mail.puts "From: #{s.user.username} <#{s.user.username}@#{Rails.application.domain}>"
93:         mail.puts "X-Is-Author: #{s.user_is_author?}"
94:         mail.puts "Reply-To: #{list}"
95:         mail.puts "To: #{list}"
96:         mail.puts "X-BeenThere: #{list}"
97:         mail.puts "List-Id: #{Rails.application.name} <#{list}>"
98:         mail.puts "List-Unsubscribe: <#{Rails.application.root_url}settings>"
99:         mail.puts "Precedence: list"
100:         mail.puts "MIME-Version: 1.0"
101:         mail.puts "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"utf-8\""
102:         mail.puts "Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable"
103:         mail.puts "Message-ID: <#{s.mailing_list_message_id}>"
104:         mail.puts "Date: " << s.created_at.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S %z")
105:         mail.puts "Subject: " << story_subject(s)
106:         mail.puts ""
107: 
108:         body = []
109: 
110:         if s.description.present?
111:           body.push s.description.to_s.word_wrap(EMAIL_WIDTH)
112:           body.push ""
113:         end
114: 
115:         body.push "Vote: #{Routes.story_short_id_url s}"
116: 
117:         if s.url.present?
118:           body.push ""
119:           body.push "Via: #{s.url}"
120: 
121:           StoryText.cached?(s) do |text|
122:             body.push ""
123:             body.push text.to_s.word_wrap(EMAIL_WIDTH)
124:           end
125:         end
126: 
127:         mail.puts body.join("\n").quoted_printable
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     last_story_id = s.id
132:   end
133: 
134:   Keystore.put(LAST_STORY_KEY, last_story_id)
135: 
136:   # repeat for comments
137: 
138:   last_comment_id = (Keystore.value_for(LAST_COMMENT_KEY) || Comment.last && Comment.last.id).to_i
139: 
140:   # Paranoia: only search back three days so that if last_comment_id is oddly low we don't start
141:   # sending every comment from the beginning of time, or if mailing list mode breaks for more than a
142:   # few days we won't bury them in email.
143:   Comment.where(
144:     "id > ? AND (is_deleted = ? AND is_moderated = ?) AND created_at >= ?",
145:     last_comment_id,
146:     false,
147:     false,
148:     3.days.ago
149:   ).order(:id).each do |c|
150:     # allow some time for newer comments to be edited before sending them out
151:     if (Time.current - c.last_edited_at) < 2.minutes
152:       break
153:     end
154: 
155:     mailing_list_users.each do |u|
156:       if u.mailing_list_mode == 2
157:         # stories only
158:         next
159:       end
160: 
161:       if (c.story.tags.map(&:id) & u.tag_filters.map(&:tag_id)).any?
162:         # story has tags this user has filtered out
163:         next
164:       end
165: 
166:       if c.story.is_hidden_by_user?(u)
167:         # user has hidden this story
168:         next
169:       end
170: 
171:       domain = Rails.application.domain
172:       list = "#{Rails.application.shortname}-#{u.mailing_list_token}@#{Rails.application.domain}"
173: 
174:       IO.popen([{}, "/usr/sbin/sendmail", "-i", "-f", "nobody@#{domain}", u.email], "w") do |mail|
175:         from = "From: \"#{c.user.username}"
176:         if c.hat
177:           from << " (#{c.hat.hat})"
178:         end
179:         from << "\" <#{c.user.username}@#{domain}>"
180:         mail.puts from
181: 
182:         mail.puts "Reply-To: #{list}"
183:         mail.puts "To: #{list}"
184:         mail.puts "List-Id: #{Rails.application.name} <#{list}>"
185:         mail.puts "List-Unsubscribe: <#{Rails.application.root_url}settings>"
186:         mail.puts "Precedence: list"
187:         mail.puts "MIME-Version: 1.0"
188:         mail.puts "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"utf-8\""
189:         mail.puts "Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable"
190:         mail.puts "Message-ID: <#{c.mailing_list_message_id}>"
191: 
192:         if c.parent_comment_id
193:           mail.puts "In-Reply-To: <#{c.parent_comment.mailing_list_message_id}>"
194:         else
195:           mail.puts "In-Reply-To: <#{c.story.mailing_list_message_id}>"
196:         end
197: 
198:         refs = ([c.story.mailing_list_message_id] +
199:           c.parents.map(&:mailing_list_message_id))
200:           .map { |r| "<#{r}>" }
201:         mail.puts "References: #{refs.join(" ")}"
202: 
203:         mail.puts "Date: " << c.created_at.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S %z")
204:         mail.puts "Subject: " << story_subject(c.story, "Re: ")
205:         mail.puts ""
206: 
207:         body = []
208: 
209:         # if the comment has hard line breaks at <80, it likely came from an
210:         # email, so don't re-wrap it at something shorter
211:         com = c.comment.to_s
212:         com_lines = com.split("\n")
213:         if com_lines.length > 1 && com_lines.first.length < 80
214:           body.push com.word_wrap(80)
215:         else
216:           body.push com.word_wrap(EMAIL_WIDTH)
217:         end
218: 
219:         body.push ""
220:         body.push "-- "
221:         body.push "Vote: #{Routes.comment_short_id_url c}"
222: 
223:         mail.puts body.join("\n").quoted_printable
224:       end
225:     end
226: 
227:     last_comment_id = c.id
228:   end
229: 
230:   Keystore.put(LAST_COMMENT_KEY, last_comment_id)
231: end

3: class Mastodon
4:   # see README.md on setting up credentials

13:   def self.accept_follow_request(id)
14:     s = Sponge.new
15:     response = s.fetch(
16:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/follow_requests/#{id}/authorize",
17:       :post,
18:       {limit: 80},
19:       nil,
20:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
21:     )
22:     raise "failed to accept follow request #{id}" if response.nil?
23:   end

25:   def self.add_list_accounts(accts)
26:     s = Sponge.new
27:     response = s.fetch(
28:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/lists/#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.list_id}/accounts",
29:       :post,
30:       nil,
31:       accts.map { |i| "account_ids[]=#{i}" }.join("&"),
32:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
33:     )
34:     raise "failed to add to list" if response.nil? || puts(response.body) || JSON.parse(response.body) != {}
35:   end
36: 
37:   def self.delete_post(story)
38:     return nil if story.mastodon_id.nil? || story.mastodon_id == "0"
39: 
40:     s = Sponge.new
41:     s.fetch(
42:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/statuses/#{story.mastodon_id}",
43:       :delete,
44:       {},
45:       nil,
46:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
47:     )
48:   end
49: 
50:   def self.follow_account(id)
51:     s = Sponge.new
52:     response = s.fetch(
53:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/accounts/#{id}/follow",
54:       :post,
55:       {reblogs: false},
56:       nil,
57:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
58:     )
59:     raise "failed to follow #{id}" if response.nil?
60:   end
61: 
62:   def self.get_account_id(acct)
63:     s = Sponge.new
64:     response = s.fetch(
65:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/accounts/search",
66:       :get,
67:       nil,
68:       {q: acct, limit: 80, resolve: true},
69:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
70:     )
71:     raise "failed to lookup #{acct}" if response.nil?
72:     accounts = JSON.parse(response.body)
73: 
74:     account = accounts.find { |a| a["acct"] == acct }
75:     # treehouse.systems is hosted at social.treehouse.systems
76:     # no idea why that's inconsistent or a better way to reconcile
77:     account = accounts.find { |a| acct.split("@").first } if account.nil?
78:     raise "did not find acct #{acct} in #{accounts}" if account.nil?
79:     account["id"]
80:   end
81: 
82:   # returns list of ids for accept_follow_request calls
83:   def self.get_follow_requests
84:     s = Sponge.new
85:     response = s.fetch(
86:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/follow_requests",
87:       :get,
88:       nil,
89:       nil,
90:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
91:     )
92:     accounts = JSON.parse(response.body)
93:     accounts.pluck("id")
94:   end
95: 
96:   # returns { "user@example.com" => 123 } for remove_list_accounts call
97:   def self.get_list_accounts
98:     s = Sponge.new
99:     response = s.fetch(
100:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/lists/#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.list_id}/accounts",
101:       :get,
102:       {limit: 0},
103:       nil,
104:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
105:     )
106:     accounts = JSON.parse(response.body)
107:     accounts.map { |a| [a["acct"], a["id"]] }.to_h
108:   end
109:

111:     s = Sponge.new
112:     s.fetch(
113:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/statuses",
114:       :post,

116:         status: status,

118:       },
119:       nil,
120:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}

122:   end

131:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}

139:       "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/accounts/#{id}/unfollow",

142:       nil,
143:       {"Authorization" => "Bearer #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.token}"}
144:     )
145:     raise "failed to remove from list" if response.nil? || JSON.parse(response.body) != {}
146:   end

148:   def self.get_bot_credentials!
149:     raise "instructions in README.md" unless Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name && Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.bot_name
150: 
151:     if !Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.client_id ||
152:         !Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.client_secret
153:       s = Sponge.new
154:       url = "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/api/v1/apps"
155:       res = s.fetch(
156:         url,
157:         :post,
158:         client_name: Rails.application.domain,

164:       )
165:       if res.nil? || res.body.blank?
166:         errors.add :base, "App registration failed, is #{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name} a Mastodon instance?"
167:         return
168:       end
169:       reg = JSON.parse(res.body)
170:       raise "no json" if !reg
171:       raise "no client_id" if reg["client_id"].blank?
172:       raise "no client_secret" if reg["client_secret"].blank?
173:     end
174: 
175:     client_id = Rails.application.credentials.client_id || reg["client_id"]
176:     client_secret = Rails.application.credentials.client_secret || reg["client_secret"]
177: 
178:     puts
179:     puts "open this URL and authorize read/write access for the bot account"
180:     puts "you'll get redirected to your site's /settings?code=..."
181:     puts "https://#{Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.instance_name}/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=#{client_id}&scope=read+write&redirect_uri=" +
182:       CGI.escape(

185:     puts

1: # This script is mostly correct but not running in production because:
2: #  1. Mastodon lists aren't public, so there's no value to it maintaining a list:
3: #     https://github.com/mastodon/mastodon/issues/8208
4: #  2. It needs more error-handling. in prod it throws errors about linked accounts that have since
5: #     been deleted, or are on instances that are down (temporarilty or permanently, etc).
6: #
7: # I'm going to leave this code alone until #1 is sorted, then think about how to handle errors:
8: # we could unlink accounts (bad for temp failures), warn in settings (good but have to figure out
9: # where to save that state), ignore (probably ok, but a waste of resources for perm failures).
10: 
11: class MastodonSyncListJob < ApplicationJob
12:   queue_as :default
13: 
14:   def perform(*args)
15:     return unless Mastodon.enabled? && Rails.application.credentials.mastodon.list_id
16: 
17:     # accept all follow requests
18:     follow_requests = Mastodon.get_follow_requests
19:     follow_requests.each do |id|
20:       Mastodon.accept_follow_request(id)
21:     end
22: 
23:     # reconcile public list with our linked accounts
24:     acct_to_id = Mastodon.get_list_accounts
25:     their_users = acct_to_id.keys.to_set
26:     our_users = Set.new(
27:       User.active.where("settings LIKE '%mastodon_username:%'")
28:         .select { |u| u.mastodon_username.present? }
29:         .map { |u| "#{u.mastodon_username}@#{u.mastodon_instance}" }
30:     )
31: 
32:     # puts "their_users", their_users
33:     # puts "our_users", our_users
34:     to_remove = their_users - our_users
35:     to_add = our_users - their_users
36: 
37:     # Mastodon requires following a user to add them to a list
38: 
39:     if to_remove.any?
40:       Mastodon.remove_list_accounts(to_remove.map { |a| acct_to_id[a] })
41:       to_remove.each do |a|
42:         Mastodon.unfollow_account(acct_to_id[a])
43:       end
44:     end
45: 
46:     if to_add.any?
47:       add_ids = []
48:       to_add.each do |a|
49:         id = Mastodon.get_account_id(a)
50:         add_ids << id
51:         Mastodon.follow_account(id)
52:       end
53:       Mastodon.add_list_accounts(add_ids)
54:     end
55:   end
56: end

3: # Comment.above_average needs to compare against the average, which is an expensive dependent
4: # subquery. CommentStat calculates and stores that once for a fast join.
5: class CommentStat < ApplicationRecord
6:   # has_many :comments # date(comments.created_at)
7: 
8:   validates :date, presence: true, uniqueness: true
9:   validates :average, presence: true
10: 
11:   # Fills daily records for the last 30 days, updating existing rows (in case the job runs don't
12:   # line up to date boundaries).
13:   def self.daily_fill!
14:     Comment.connection.execute <<~SQL
15:       insert low_priority into comment_stats (`date`, `average`)
16:       with avg_by_date as (
17:         select
18:           date(created_at - interval 5 hour) as date, avg(score) as a
19:         from comments
20:         where
21:           (comments.created_at - interval 5 hour) >= now() - interval 30 day and
22:           comments.is_deleted = false
23:         group by date(created_at - interval 5 hour)
24:       )
25:       select date, a from avg_by_date
26:       on duplicate key update `average` = `a`
27:     SQL
28:   end
29: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: # Finds the consistent most-heavily-flagged commenters. Requires flags to be spread over
4: # several comments and stories because anyone can have a bad thread or a bad day.
5: 
6: class FlaggedCommenters
7:   include IntervalHelper
8: 
9:   attr_reader :interval, :period, :cache_time
10: 
11:   def initialize(interval, cache_time = 30.minutes)
12:     @interval = interval
13:     @cache_time = cache_time
14:     length = time_interval(interval)
15:     @period = length[:dur].send(length[:intv].downcase).ago
16:   end
17: 
18:   def check_list_for(showing_user)
19:     commenters[showing_user.id]
20:   end
21: 
22:   # aggregates for all commenters; not just those receiving flags
23:   def aggregates
24:     Rails.cache.fetch("aggregates_#{interval}_#{cache_time}", expires_in: cache_time) {
25:       ActiveRecord::Base.connection.exec_query("
26:         select
27:           stddev(sum_flags) as stddev,
28:           sum(sum_flags) as sum,
29:           avg(sum_flags) as avg,
30:           avg(n_comments) as n_comments,
31:           count(*) as n_commenters
32:         from (
33:           select
34:             sum(flags) as sum_flags,
35:             count(*) as n_comments
36:           from comments join users on comments.user_id = users.id
37:           where
38:             (comments.created_at >= '#{period}') and
39:             users.banned_at is null and
40:             users.deleted_at is null
41:           GROUP BY comments.user_id
42:         ) sums;
43:       ").first.symbolize_keys!
44:     }
45:   end
46: 
47:   def stddev_sum_flags
48:     aggregates[:stddev].to_f
49:   end
50: 
51:   def avg_sum_flags
52:     aggregates[:avg].to_f
53:   end
54: 
55:   def commenters
56:     Rails.cache.fetch("flagged_commenters_#{interval}_#{cache_time}",
57:       expires_in: cache_time) {
58:       rank = 0
59:       User.active.joins(:comments)
60:         .where("comments.created_at >= ?", period)
61:         .group("comments.user_id")
62:         .select("
63:           users.id, users.username,
64:           (sum(flags) - #{avg_sum_flags})/#{stddev_sum_flags} as sigma,
65:           count(distinct if(flags > 0, comments.id, null)) as n_comments,
66:           count(distinct if(flags > 0, story_id, null)) as n_stories,
67:           sum(flags) as n_flags,
68:           sum(flags)/count(distinct comments.id) as average_flags,
69:           (
70:             count(distinct if(flags > 0, comments.id, null)) /
71:             count(distinct comments.id)
72:           ) * 100 as percent_flagged")
73:         .having("n_comments > 4 and n_stories > 1 and n_flags >= 10 and percent_flagged > 10")
74:         .order(sigma: :desc)
75:         .limit(30)
76:         .each_with_object({}) { |u, hash|
77:           hash[u.id] = {
78:             username: u.username,
79:             rank: rank += 1,
80:             sigma: u.sigma,
81:             n_comments: u.n_comments,
82:             n_stories: u.n_stories,
83:             n_flags: u.n_flags,
84:             average_flags: u.average_flags,
85:             stddev: 0,
86:             percent_flagged: u.percent_flagged
87:           }
88:         }
89:     }
90:   end
91: end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # typed: false
2: 
3: # The unique value to identify an Origin is 'identifier', not the tuple (domain, identifier).
4: # Origin.domain is set from the identifier to support sharing an Origin between Domains. The URLs
5: # foo.github.io and github.com/foo have two different Domains that both produce the Origin with
6: # identifier github.com/foo. That Origin's domain is set to github.com.
7: class Origin < ApplicationRecord
8:   belongs_to :domain
9:   has_many :stories
10:   belongs_to :banned_by_user,
11:     class_name: "User",
12:     inverse_of: false,
13:     optional: true
14:   has_one :moderation, dependent: :restrict_with_exception
15: 
16:   validates :identifier, presence: true, length: {maximum: 255}, uniqueness: {case_sensitive: false}
17:   validates :stories_count, numericality: {only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0}, presence: true
18:   validates :banned_reason, length: {maximum: 200}
19: 
20:   include Token
21: 
22:   # weird that this isn't automatic for new records
23:   after_create { Origin.reset_counters(id, :stories) }
24: 
25:   def self./(identifier)
26:     find_by! identifier:
27:   end
28: 
29:   def ban_by_user_for_reason!(banner, reason)
30:     self.banned_at = Time.current
31:     self.banned_by_user_id = banner.id
32:     self.banned_reason = reason
33:     save!
34: 
35:     m = Moderation.new
36:     m.moderator_user_id = banner.id
37:     m.origin = self
38:     m.action = "Banned"
39:     m.reason = reason
40:     m.save!
41:   end
42: 
43:   def unban_by_user_for_reason!(banner, reason)
44:     self.banned_at = nil
45:     self.banned_by_user_id = nil
46:     self.banned_reason = nil
47:     save!
48: 
49:     m = Moderation.new
50:     m.moderator_user_id = banner.id
51:     m.origin = self
52:     m.action = "Unbanned"
53:     m.reason = reason
54:     m.save!
55:   end
56: 
57:   def banned?
58:     banned_at?
59:   end
60: 
61:   def n_submitters
62:     stories.count("distinct user_id")
63:   end
64: 
65:   def to_param
66:     identifier
67:   end
68: end
